title: Berlin Heatwave Day Plan - Quick Summary
items: **Do the heat-warning check first.** Berlin’s heat-protection portal points to German Weather Service warnings and practical district measures.
**Do not treat the U-Bahn as an air-conditioned break.** BVG says Berlin U-Bahn trains use ventilation, but they do not have air conditioning.
**Use the heat rhythm: morning outside, midday cooling, evening outside again.** Exposed squares and long walks are much easier before 11:00 or after 18:00.
**Carry a refillable bottle and refill early.** Use the Berliner Wasserbetriebe drinking-fountain map before a long walk, U-Bahn ride or lake/pool trip.
**Make lakes and pools part of the plan, not a panic backup.** Check official water quality, tickets and current opening hours before leaving.
